name: The Concertina Wire — Draw Approach
description: Three rolls of German concertina wire staked into the sand at the base of the Vierville Draw, each roll interlaced with the next, forming a barrier twelve feet deep that no man can cross without being cut to pieces or cut through with explosives.
atmosphericDetails: The wire catches the morning light in small bright points along its length — it looks almost decorative from a distance.
The dead zone to the left of the wire is identifiable by the absence of fire — a fifteen-yard strip of sand that the MG-42 cannot reach due to the bunker's concrete embrasure.
The draw rises steeply behind the wire, its grass sides channeling the wind into a low moan that sounds, at intervals, almost human.
